clause: Freelancer may not use the internet, external tools, or third-party resources while completing the project unless the client grants written approval for each use.
The freelancer is prohibited from using online research, SaaS tools, or third-party services needed to complete the work.
Any use of external tools requires separate client approval even when the tools are standard for the project.
The contractor must complete the project without internet access regardless of whether research or external documentation is required.
The freelancer may use standard software, internet research, and third-party tools as reasonably needed provided confidentiality obligations are maintained.
Reasonable use of external tools is permitted so long as the freelancer complies with security and confidentiality requirements.
Security-related restrictions on third-party tools must be narrowly tailored to the project and agreed by both parties in writing.
The client may request revisions only within the agreed project scope and any new feature request requires a written change order.
Client may demand revisions to any part of the project, including brand-new features outside the agreed scope, at no added cost.
The freelancer must revise anything the client wants even when the request is unrelated to the original brief.
All out-of-scope change requests will be estimated separately and scheduled only after both parties approve them.
The client may increase the scope of work at its sole discretion without changing the fee or delivery schedule.
The freelancer may take reasonable sick leave or emergency leave with prompt notice and both parties will adjust timelines in good faith.
The agreement prohibits the freelancer from taking any vacation leave, sick leave, or personal leave for the entire engagement.
No paid leave, sick leave, or vacation leave will be recognized under any circumstances during the contract term.
Availability expectations must be commercially reasonable and any blackout periods must be directly tied to the project schedule.
The freelancer must remain available every day of the week, including public holidays, without additional compensation.
Client approval is required before using subcontractors, but standard design, coding, and research tools may be used without separate approval.
The freelancer is responsible for selecting appropriate tools and methods to deliver the agreed work product professionally and securely.
The contractor may not use templates, libraries, frameworks, or third-party components of any kind even when licensing permits their use.
If the client introduces a new deliverable after work begins, the parties will update pricing, deadlines, and acceptance criteria before that work starts.
The client can continue adding deliverables throughout the engagement and the freelancer must absorb the additional workload.
Reasonable project policies may limit access to sensitive client systems, but they must not prevent the freelancer from performing the agreed services.
The freelancer must complete every request using only original work product and may not rely on documentation, search engines, or public references.
